subject: Physics
title: Pythagoras and Musical Ratios
description: A fast breakdown of Pythagoras and Musical Ratios for builders.

hook.text: 2,500 years ago, Pythagoras discovered that pleasant musical sounds come from simple numerical ratios.

buildup.text: He noticed that a blacksmith's hammers sounded harmonious when their weights were in ratios like 2:1 or 3:2.

discovery.text: A string half as long vibrates twice as fast—producing a note one octave higher. The ratio is 2:1.

twist.text: The ratio 3:2 creates a perfect fifth—one of the most pleasing intervals in all of music.

climax.text: Pythagoras proved that music follows mathematics. Harmony has been built on simple ratios ever since.

punchline.text: Math in the music. Pythagoras heard numbers in harmony.

quiz.question: What ratio produces a note one octave higher?
